statement: Polling data consistently shows that support for EU membership in Iceland is higher among younger voters (18–34: ~55% in favour), urban residents in the capital region (~50%), and those with university education (~52%). Opposition is strongest among older voters (55+: ~50% opposed), rural residents (~55% opposed), and those employed in agriculture and fisheries (~65% opposed).
source_name: Félagsvísindastofnun Háskóla Íslands — Social Science Research Institute
caveats: Demographic breakdowns in Icelandic polls have smaller sample sizes per subgroup (total population ~390,000), increasing margin of error for individual categories. Cross-tabulations should be interpreted with caution.

related_entries.statement: Gallup polls conducted in Iceland between 2022 and early 2026 show a gradual shift in public opinion on EU membership. Support for applying for EU membership rose from approximately 30% in 2022 to around 45% by late 2025, while opposition declined from roughly 50% to 40%. The remainder were undecided. The trend accelerated following the Althingi's decision in 2024 to hold a binding referendum.

citations.report_title: Ræða þarf alla kaflana á nýjan leik

claims.claim_text: Margir sem studdu ESB-aðild vildu fá evru í stað íslensku krónunnar.

claims.claim_text: Íbúar höfuðborgarsvæðisins eru frekar hlynntir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu en íbúar landsbyggðarinnar.
Fólk með háskólamenntun er frekar hlynnt þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu en fólk með minni menntun.
Íbúar höfuðborgarsvæðisins myndu frekar kjósa með framhaldi aðildarviðræðna en íbúar landsbyggðarinnar.
Fólk með háskólamenntun myndi frekar kjósa með framhaldi aðildarviðræðna en fólk með minni menntun.
